content:
Hi! I am a senior student at KAIST co-majoring in Computer Science and Brain Engineering. Currently advised by professor Seunghoon Hong, I am studying artificial intelligence and computer vision. I am particularly interested in learning visual concepts with less data, and learning to reason about objects and their interactions in a scene. Previously, I was advised by professor Se-Bum Paik and participated in a computational neuroscience research as a co-first author. Our work addresses the development of visual circuits in absence of experience, and is now published in The Journal of Neuroscience.
Apart from work, I enjoy listening to music - and composing them as well! My favorite musicians include Radiohead, Carpenters, eAeon, and Ryuichi Sakamoto. My friends and I are running an independent composer group named Regular Daydream Club (정기적 멍때리기 클럽), collaborating with webcomic artist Ju Hwan Lee.

I am particularly interested in learning visual concepts with less data, and learning to reason about objects and their interactions in a scene.
title: Jinwoo Kim
Spontaneous retinal waves can generate long-range horizontal connectivity in visual cortex
